game_name: The Mafat Conspiracy: Golgo 13 Part II

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites with visible weapons (pistols, machine guns, grenades, scythes, boomerangs, knives)
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Scorpion and dog sprites in combat sections
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental/biological threats mixed with human adversaries

danger_signals.visual_form: Time-pressure UI on horizontally scrolling, stationary, and maze screens
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; failure state if time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: Mafat hideout and base locations; FSB agent presence in Ferrari chase sections
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy-controlled zones where Golgo 13 is under constant threat

safety_signals.visual_form: James (male CIA agent) and Sylvia (female CIA agent) as NPCs providing information and weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Allied faction presence; source of resources and mission intelligence

safety_signals.visual_form: Ammo and equipment scattered throughout scrolling/action stages
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve and resource availability; encourages exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Infrared scope item in maze sections enabling night vision
safety_signals.meaning: Upgrade availability; progression toward mission objectives

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Arm of Mafat (hideout unit), General bodyguard (protects Ahmad Khan), Canine handler (Mafat base sentry); standard foot soldiers with varied weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mafat hideout in Paris, Mafat base locations; satellite capture system installation
James and Sylvia agent presence; Dr. Barrows extraction mission objective; Los Angeles submarine reference (hostage leverage)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: FSB agents in Ferrari chase sequences; treated as adversaries despite being Cold War counterpart to CIA
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in vehicle action sections; blamed in extortion conflict; electromagnetic wave research as leverage asset

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Global nuclear threat (satellite fell from orbit)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sniper rifle aiming mechanic as primary assassination tool; emphasis on precision strikes and elimination missions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Golgo 13 must complete precision objectives (eliminate Mafat leader, destroy satellite capture system) rather than generic combat progression

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Extortion crisis (Mafat demanding surrender of military assets)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: CIA vs. FSB conflict represented through dual-agent adversary types; mutual blame dynamic visible in mission briefings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates geopolitical tension through stages set across London and Paris; must work with CIA while avoiding FSB operatives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dr. Barrows as mission objective marker; locations (London lab → Paris captivity) establish spatial narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Linear mission progression structured around rescue objective; James and Sylvia provide location intelligence for hostage recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 5 distinct screen types (scrolling combat, stationary brawl, Ferrari chase, maze navigation, sniper scope) representing different operational environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay shifts between stealth/precision (sniper), mobility (Ferrari), infiltration (maze), and direct combat (scrolling/stationary)

environmental_storytelling.example: Mission geography flows London (Dr. Barrows abduction site) → Paris (captivity location) → Various Mafat facilities, establishing temporal and spatial hostage narrative
Specific enemy rosters tied to locations: hideout features 'Arm of Mafat,' base features 'Canine' sentry, Paris operations feature varied weapons suggesting fortress preparation
CIA agents provide mission support while FSB operatives appear as independent threats in Ferrari sections, visually communicating Cold War-era distrust and competing interests
Satellite capture system as visible mission target; maze sections contain infrared scope (upgrade for night operations); sniper screens isolate target location in extreme closeup

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can shoot, kick, punch, throw grenades, drive vehicles, snipe—behavioral flexibility reflects spy/assassin multi-discipline training
Attack with ranged and melee weapons; indicate active defense posture of terrorist organization
Chase and attack from vehicles; indicate Cold War-era military opposition and geopolitical adversity
Provides mission intelligence and weapons; indicates allied faction resource provision
Provides assistance (specific mechanics undefined in source); indicates secondary allied support structure
Stationary objective; extraction target driving mission narrative

notes: The Mafat Conspiracy uses Cold War espionage framing (CIA vs. FSB mutual suspicion, nuclear satellite threat, military hardware extortion) to establish world-state. Visual communication relies on enemy type variety (weapons, animals, ranked operators like 'Arm of Mafat'), screen-type switching to reflect operational environment changes (scrolling urban combat → enclosed maze infiltration → sniper precision), and faction presence through allied agents (CIA) and opposing forces (FSB, Mafat). The game lacks explicit color-coding or symbolic faction identifiers documented in available sources; faction identity is communicated through mission role (ally vs. adversary) and weapon type rather than visual markers. Danger is communicated through enemy density, weapon visibility, and time pressure. Safety through resource availability (ammo, equipment) and allied NPC presence.
